question: Our family of 4: two adults, two kids (ages 6 and 9). The 6-year-old is allergic to milk. What can each person order?
My wife and I want burgers. She is gluten‑intolerant. I have no restrictions.
Three friends: one vegan, one allergic to nuts, one wants a high protein burger. Suggestions?
Couple with a 5-year-old. The child is vegetarian. The mother is lactose intolerant. Father has no restrictions.
A group of 4 colleagues: two have gluten allergy, one is vegan, one wants low calorie. Help us order.
Family of 5: grandparents, parents, and a 7-year-old. The child has a peanut allergy. Grandfather is diabetic (low sugar). Others no restrictions.
Two friends: one is on keto (low carb), the other is vegetarian. Can we order from the same menu?
Mom and dad with two teenagers (14 and 16). The younger teen is allergic to eggs. Everyone else fine.
A couple with a toddler (2 years). Toddler can't have dairy. Wife is gluten‑free. Husband wants a double cheeseburger.
Four sports teammates: two need high protein, one is vegan, one has a fish allergy. What do we order?
Parents with two kids (8 and 10). The 8-year-old is celiac (gluten‑free). The 10-year-old hates onions. Mother is vegetarian. Father no restrictions.
A group of friends: one lactose intolerant, one allergic to sesame, one on a low carb diet, one wants a shake. Help!
Wedding party of 10: we have a mix of vegetarians, vegans, gluten‑free, and nut allergies. Too complex, but can you suggest a few items that work for many?
Dad and two sons (12 and 15). The younger son is allergic to milk. The older son wants a spicy chicken sandwich. Dad wants a quarter pounder.
Office lunch for 5: one person is vegan, one is gluten‑free, one has a shellfish allergy, two are fine. Can we order a mix?
A mother with three children (4, 7, 10). The 4-year-old is lactose intolerant. The 7-year-old doesn't like pickles. The 10-year-old wants a burger with everything. Mother is gluten‑free.
Two couples: one couple is vegetarian, the other is not. One person has a nut allergy. How to order?
Family of 6: grandparents, parents, two kids. Grandma has celiac (gluten‑free). Grandpa has a fish allergy. Kids are picky, no onions. Parents fine.
A rugby team of 8: most want high protein, two are vegetarian, one has a milk allergy. Suggestions?
Our family of 3: mom, dad, 8-year-old son. Son is allergic to milk. Dad wants a Big Mac. Mom wants a salad with no cheese. What can we order?
Two friends: Alice is vegetarian, Bob has a peanut allergy. Alice wants a veggie burger. Bob wants a chicken sandwich. What's safe?
A couple: John is gluten‑intolerant, Mary is lactose intolerant. John wants a burger without bun. Mary wants a milkshake but can't have milk. Help!
Family of 4: parents, two kids (6 and 10). The 6-year-old has a fish allergy. The 10-year-old hates onions. Parents want spicy chicken sandwiches. What to order?
Three colleagues: one vegan, one on keto (low carb), one with a sesame allergy. They want to share a meal. Any common items?
Mom and two daughters (14 and 17). The 14-year-old is vegetarian. The 17-year-old is allergic to eggs. Mom wants a low calorie meal. Any options?
A group of 5 friends: two are gluten‑free, one is vegan, one has a nut allergy, one wants a high protein meal. Can we find something for everyone?
Grandparents with their grandson (5). Grandfather is diabetic (low sugar). Grandmother has a milk allergy. Grandson wants chicken nuggets. What's safe?
Two roommates: one is vegetarian, the other is allergic to soy. They want to order pizza (but no pizza), so burgers. Any suggestions?

Family of 5: parents, three kids (4, 7, 9). The 4-year-old is lactose intolerant. The 7-year-old hates pickles. The 9-year-old wants a McFlurry but is allergic to nuts. Parents want spicy chicken sandwiches.
A couple celebrating anniversary: wife is gluten‑free, husband is allergic to shellfish. Wife wants a pasta (not available), but a salad. Husband wants a fish sandwich. Any safe fish?
Three sisters: one vegan, one low carb, one has a sesame allergy. They want to share fries and drinks. What's common?
Dad with two teenagers (13 and 16). The 13-year-old is vegetarian. The 16-year-old is allergic to milk. Dad wants a double cheeseburger. What to order?
A group of 8 from a soccer team: most want high protein, two are vegetarian, one has a nut allergy, one is gluten‑free. Any suggestions for the team?
Family of 3: single mom, son (10), daughter (14). Son is allergic to eggs. Daughter is on a low carb diet. Mom wants a salad with no cheese.
Two coworkers: one has celiac (gluten‑free), the other is lactose intolerant. They want a quick lunch. Any sandwich without gluten and milk?
A mother with her teenage son (15). Son is allergic to peanuts. Mother is vegan. Son wants a burger with bacon. Mother wants a veggie burger. Any safe options?
Grandparents with two grandchildren (6 and 8). The 6-year-old has a fish allergy. The 8-year-old hates ketchup. Grandparents are fine. What to order?
A couple with a baby (1 year). Baby is too young for solids, just milk formula (not our concern). Mom is vegetarian. Dad wants a spicy chicken sandwich. Mom wants a vegetarian wrap.
Four university students: one vegan, one low carb, one allergic to sesame, one wants a milkshake. They need a meal that works for all? Probably not, but separate orders.
Father and daughter (12). Daughter is allergic to milk. Father is on a keto diet (low carb). Daughter wants a cheeseburger but can't have cheese. Father wants a bunless burger.
A group of 6 from a book club: three are vegetarian, one has a nut allergy, one is gluten‑free, one loves fries. They want to share a platter (but no platter). How to order individually?
Mom and her two sons (5 and 9). The 5-year-old has a milk allergy. The 9-year-old hates onions. Mom wants a salad with no cheese. Any options?
A couple: one is pescatarian (fish but no meat), the other is allergic to fish. That's tricky. Any common ground?
Family of 3: dad, mom, 7-year-old. The 7-year-old is celiac (gluten‑free). Mom is vegetarian. Dad wants a quarter pounder. Any safe meal for the child?
Two friends: one is on a paleo diet (no grains, no dairy), the other is vegan. They want to share a meal. Any common items?
A mother with her teenage daughter (15). Daughter is allergic to eggs. Mother is on a low fat diet. Daughter wants a McMuffin with egg? Not possible. Mother wants a grilled chicken sandwich.
Group of 3 seniors: one has diabetes (low sugar), one has high blood pressure (low sodium), one has a gluten allergy. Any items that suit all?
A family with a child with a nut allergy and a parent with a fish allergy. The other parent is vegetarian. The child wants chicken nuggets. What's safe?
Two business partners: one is gluten‑free and vegetarian, the other is low carb and has a milk allergy. They need a quick meeting lunch.
A mom with her 6-year-old son. Son is allergic to milk and eggs. Mom is vegan. Son wants a happy meal. Any happy meal without milk and eggs?
A family of 4: parents, two kids (7 and 9). The 7-year-old has a peanut allergy. The 9-year-old is vegetarian. Parents want burgers with everything.
Two roommates: one is on a whole30 diet (no grains, no dairy, no sugar), the other is allergic to shellfish. What can they order together?
A couple: man is low carb, woman is vegetarian and allergic to sesame. They want to share a pizza? No pizza, but burgers. Any?
Mom and her two kids (8 and 11). The 8-year-old has a soy allergy. The 11-year-old is gluten‑free. Mom wants a salad with no cheese. Suggestions?
Three friends: one is keto, one is vegetarian, one has a milk allergy. They want to get ice cream? Not possible. Maybe shakes? Not for the milk allergy. Any dessert?
A father with his 16-year-old son. Son is allergic to peanuts and wants a burger. Father is gluten‑free and wants a salad. Any safe burger for son?
A family of 5: grandparents, parents, child (10). Grandfather has a fish allergy. Grandmother is diabetic (low sugar). Child is vegetarian. Parents have no restrictions. Help!
Two friends: one is paleo, one is vegan. They want to go for a burger. Any burger that fits both? Possibly a lettuce wrap with no cheese, no sauce, but vegan? Maybe not.
A mom with her 5-year-old. Child has a milk allergy. Mom is on a low calorie diet. Child wants a happy meal. Any happy meal without milk?
A group of 4 from a gym: two want high protein, one is vegetarian, one has a nut allergy. They want a post‑workout meal.
A couple with a toddler (2). Toddler is allergic to eggs. Mother is gluten‑free. Father wants a double cheeseburger. What can the toddler eat?
Three office workers: one has celiac (gluten‑free), one is low carb, one has a milk allergy. They need a quick lunch that fits all.
A family of 4: mom, dad, 12-year-old, 14-year-old. The 12-year-old is allergic to fish. The 14-year-old is vegetarian. Mom wants a spicy chicken wrap. Dad wants a Big Mac.
Two friends: one is on a low FODMAP diet (no onion, no garlic, no wheat), the other is vegetarian. They want to share a meal.
A mother with her 4-year-old. Child is allergic to soy. Mother is vegan. Child wants chicken nuggets. Chicken nuggets contain soy? Possibly. What to do?
A group of 5 from a hiking club: one is gluten‑free, one is low carb, one is vegetarian, one has a nut allergy, one is fine. Any meal for all?
A dad with his two kids (5 and 7). The 5-year-old has a milk allergy. The 7-year-old hates pickles. Dad wants a spicy chicken sandwich. Any safe options for the kids?
Two friends: one is on a low sodium diet (heart condition), the other is allergic to sesame. They want a quick lunch.
A family of 3: single dad, son (9), daughter (12). Son is allergic to eggs. Daughter is vegetarian. Dad wants a quarter pounder. What can the kids eat?
A couple: one is on a keto diet (low carb), the other is allergic to milk. They want to share a dessert. Any keto dessert without milk?
A mother with her 8-year-old son. Son is allergic to fish. Mother is gluten‑free. Son wants a Filet‑O‑Fish? Not safe. Mother wants a salad. Any fish‑free for son?
A group of 4 from a yoga class: two are vegan, one is gluten‑free, one has a nut allergy. They want a light meal after yoga.
A father with his teenage son (14). Son is allergic to peanuts. Father is on a low calorie diet. Son wants a burger with bacon. Father wants a salad.
Two friends: one is a vegetarian, one is allergic to gluten. They want to order fries and a burger. Any burger without gluten for the second?
A mom with her two daughters (7 and 10). The 7-year-old has a milk allergy. The 10-year-old hates lettuce. Mom wants a spicy chicken wrap. Any options?
Our family: me (no restrictions), my wife (vegetarian), son 5 (lactose intolerant). What can we order?
Two colleagues: one is vegan, the other has celiac disease. What do you recommend for lunch?
Family of 3: dad (loves spicy), mom (gluten‑free), daughter 8 (allergic to milk). Help us pick a meal.
Two friends: one is on a keto diet (low carb), the other is vegetarian and allergic to eggs. What can we share?
A mother with two teens: one teen is vegan, the other has a nut allergy. Mother wants a low calorie meal. Any suggestions?
Parents with a 10-year-old: the child is gluten‑intolerant. Father is lactose intolerant. Mother has no restrictions. What can we order together?
Group of 6 friends: three are vegetarian, one is vegan, one has a fish allergy, one is lactose intolerant. We want a mix of meals. Help!
Grandparents and two grandchildren (6 and 8). Grandpa has a peanut allergy. Grandmother is diabetic (low sugar). Kids have no restrictions.
A couple: husband is on a low carb diet, wife wants a high protein meal. They also need a kids meal for their 4-year-old who is allergic to milk.
Three sisters: one is vegetarian and hates mayo, one is gluten‑free, one is allergic to eggs. Order for all.
Office lunch for 4: one is vegan, one has a sesame allergy, one wants a low calorie salad, one wants a double cheeseburger. Can you assist?
Family of 5: father (no restrictions), mother (lactose intolerant), son 12 (celiac), daughter 9 (vegetarian), daughter 4 (allergic to nuts). What can we order?
Two couples: one couple is vegetarian, the other couple is on a low carb diet. Both couples have a child with a milk allergy. This is complicated.
A father with his three kids (6, 9, 12). The youngest is allergic to eggs. The middle is vegetarian. The oldest wants a spicy chicken sandwich. Dad wants a Big Mac.
Mixed friend group: one vegan, one gluten-free, one nut allergy, one lactose intolerant, one wants high protein, one low calorie. Six people. Help!
Mom and dad with a 7-year-old and a baby (2). The 7-year-old is vegetarian. The baby is lactose intolerant. Parents have no restrictions.
Group of 5 construction workers: three want high protein, one is allergic to gluten, one is diabetic (low sugar). They all want burgers.
A mother with two daughters (14 and 17). The younger is vegan. The older has a sesame allergy. Mother is gluten-free. What can they order?
Grandparents with three grandchildren (5,7,9). Grandpa is allergic to milk. Grandma is vegetarian. The 7-year-old hates pickles. The 9-year-old wants extra cheese. The 5-year-old needs low sodium.
A soccer team of 9: many want high protein, two are vegetarian, one has a fish allergy, one is vegan. Suggest a group order.
Family of 4: dad (wants a low carb meal), mom (allergic to milk), son 10 (gluten-free), daughter 6 (vegetarian). What can we order for everyone?
Two sisters: one is vegan and allergic to soy, the other is gluten-free and wants a high protein meal. Suggestions for both?
A widowed father with two teenage boys (14 and 16). The father is diabetic (low sugar). The boys want high protein, no restrictions. Order help.
Three generations: grandfather (low sodium), grandmother (gluten-free), mother (lactose intolerant), father (no restrictions), two kids (5 and 8) both like chicken nuggets. Help us order.
A group of friends on a road trip: one is keto, one vegetarian, one allergic to peanuts, one wants a milkshake (no allergies).
Parents with a 12-year-old and a 6-month-old baby (no solid food). The 12-year-old is allergic to eggs. Mother is vegan. Father has no restrictions.
Office team of 6: two vegans, one gluten-free, one nut allergy, one high protein, one low calorie. We need to order together. Is that possible?
A family of 4: mom (gluten‑free), dad (lactose intolerant), son 15 (wants a spicy burger), daughter 9 (vegetarian). What can we order?
Group of 8 people: 4 want low calorie, 2 are vegetarian, 1 is vegan, 1 has a fish allergy. We're ordering for a party.
A single father with a 7-year-old boy who is allergic to milk and eggs. The father is on a low carb diet. Help them order dinner.
Two couples with a child each: couple A is vegetarian, their child (3) is allergic to milk. Couple B has no restrictions, their child (6) is gluten‑intolerant. Suggest meals.
Grandparents and two grandchildren (5 and 7). Grandma has a nut allergy. Grandpa is diabetic. Kids have no restrictions. Order for them.
A group of 7 college students: three are vegan, two are gluten‑free, one is lactose intolerant, one wants a burger with everything. Help us order.
Mother with two daughters (10 and 13). The 10-year-old is allergic to gluten. The 13-year-old hates onions. Mother is on a low calorie diet. What can we order?
A father with his son (16) and daughter (12). The son wants a high protein meal. The daughter is vegetarian. The father has a fish allergy. Suggestions.
Three siblings: one is vegan, one is allergic to milk, one wants a low carb meal. What can we order for dinner?
A pregnant woman with her husband and 4-year-old. The woman has gestational diabetes (low sugar). The husband wants a spicy burger. The child is lactose intolerant. Help.
Five seniors from a retirement home: two have high blood pressure (low sodium), one is diabetic, one has a gluten allergy, one has no restrictions. Order for them.
Mother and her two children (8 and 11). The 8-year-old is allergic to soy. The 11-year-old is vegetarian. Mother wants a low fat meal. Help us order.
A father with his two sons (5 and 9). The 5-year-old is allergic to eggs. The 9-year-old is gluten‑free. The father wants a double cheeseburger. Suggestions.
Group of 9: we have a mix of low carb, vegan, vegetarian, gluten‑free, nut allergy, fish allergy, low calorie, high protein, and one person who eats anything. What do we do?
A mother with a teenage son (15) and daughter (13). The son is allergic to milk. The daughter is on a low carb diet. Mother is gluten‑free. Order for the family.
Two families with kids: Family A (dad, mom, 6yo) – mom vegetarian, child allergic to nuts. Family B (dad, mom, 8yo) – dad gluten‑free, child lactose intolerant. Combine order.
A couple celebrating anniversary: wife is vegan, husband is allergic to sesame. They want a nice meal. Suggest options.
A father with his three daughters (5, 8, 12). The 5-year-old is allergic to milk. The 8-year-old is vegetarian. The 12-year-old wants a spicy chicken sandwich. Father wants a burger.
Three roommates: one is keto, one is vegan, one has a gluten allergy. They need to order delivery. Help them choose.
Grandmother and grandfather with two grandchildren (4 and 6). Grandmother is allergic to fish. Grandfather has a nut allergy. Kids have no restrictions. What can they all eat?
A family of 5: dad (low carb), mom (vegetarian), son 10 (gluten‑free), daughter 7 (allergic to milk), baby 1 (lactose intolerant). We need a meal plan.
Group of 10 from a gym: 6 want high protein, 2 are vegetarian, 1 is vegan, 1 has a milk allergy. Order to satisfy everyone.
A mother with her two kids (5 and 9). The 5-year-old is allergic to eggs. The 9-year-old hates pickles. Mother is on a low calorie diet. Help them order lunch.
Father and two sons (11 and 13). The 11-year-old is vegetarian. The 13-year-old is allergic to nuts. Father wants a quarter pounder. Suggestions for everyone.
A couple with a 7-year-old and a 10-year-old. The 7-year-old is gluten‑free. The 10-year-old is lactose intolerant. Mother is vegan. Father wants a spicy burger. What can they order?
Three friends: one is keto, one is vegetarian, one has a fish allergy. They want to share a meal but also order individually. Help them decide.
A mother with three children (4, 6, 9). The 4-year-old is allergic to milk. The 6-year-old is vegetarian. The 9-year-old wants a McFlurry. Mother is gluten‑free. Order for the family.
Two colleagues: one has a peanut allergy, the other is on a low calorie diet. They want to order lunch together. What options do they have?
A grandfather with his grandson (8). The grandfather is diabetic (low sugar). The grandson is allergic to milk. Order for them.
A family of 6: three adults, three kids (4, 7, 10). The 4-year-old is allergic to eggs. The 7-year-old is vegetarian. The 10-year-old hates onions. The adults have no restrictions. Help them order.
Four friends: one vegan, one gluten‑free, one low carb, one wants a normal meal. They're going to a McDonald's. Help them choose.
A mother with her 12-year-old son and 15-year-old daughter. The son is allergic to milk. The daughter is on a low carb diet. Mother wants a low calorie meal. Help.
A father with his two sons (6 and 9). The 6-year-old is allergic to soy. The 9-year-old is gluten‑free. The father wants a bacon burger. Order for them.
Three sisters: one vegan, one lactose intolerant, one on a low carb diet. They want to share a meal. What can they order together?
A grandmother with her two grandchildren (5 and 8). The grandmother has a nut allergy. The 5-year-old is vegetarian. The 8-year-old wants a cheeseburger. Help them order.
A family of 5: father (low carb), mother (gluten‑free), son 14 (high protein), daughter 10 (vegetarian), daughter 6 (allergic to milk). Order for everyone.
A group of friends: two are keto, one is vegan, one has a shellfish allergy, one is gluten‑free, one wants a dessert. This is complex. Suggest a few items that might work for multiple.
A mother with her 9-year-old daughter who is allergic to milk and eggs. The mother wants a high protein meal. Order for them.

A father and his three kids (4, 7, 10). The 4-year-old is allergic to milk. The 7-year-old is vegetarian. The 10-year-old wants a spicy chicken sandwich. Father wants a double cheeseburger. What can they order?
Two mothers with their daughters (both 5). One mother is vegan, the other is gluten‑free. One daughter is allergic to nuts, the other is lactose intolerant. Order for the group.
A couple: the husband is on a low carb diet, the wife is allergic to milk. They have a 3-year-old who is vegetarian. Help them order.

A mother with a teenage daughter (14). The daughter is vegan. The mother is lactose intolerant. They want to order a meal together. Suggestions.
Two friends: one is on a keto diet, the other has a fish allergy. They want to order burgers. What can they both eat safely?
A grandfather with his grandson (6). The grandfather has a low sodium diet. The grandson is allergic to milk. Order for them.
A family of 4: dad (low carb), mom (vegetarian), son 7 (gluten‑free), daughter 5 (allergic to eggs). What can they order for dinner?
Three roommates: one is vegan, one has a nut allergy, one wants a low calorie meal. They need to order for the evening. Help.
A father with his two daughters (10 and 12). The 10-year-old is allergic to milk. The 12-year-old is on a low carb diet. Father wants a quarter pounder with cheese. Order for them.
A mother with her son (7) and daughter (9). The son is gluten‑free. The daughter is vegetarian. Mother wants a high protein salad. What can they order?
Two couples: Couple A (both vegetarian, one has a peanut allergy). Couple B (both gluten‑free, one wants low carb). Help them order.
A grandmother with her three grandchildren (3, 6, 9). The 3-year-old is allergic to milk. The 6-year-old is vegetarian. The 9-year-old hates onions. Grandmother is diabetic. Order for them.
A father with his son (8). The son is allergic to eggs. The father is on a low carb diet. What can they order together?

A grandfather with his granddaughter (5). The grandfather has a nut allergy. The granddaughter is allergic to milk. What can they order?
